features.title: Convert in bulk
features.body: Queue as many PDFs as you like. Each is converted to clean Markdown, one after another, right in your browser.

faqs.q: How many PDFs can I convert at once?
faqs.a: There's no fixed limit — add as many as you like. Files are processed one at a time in your browser, so very large batches simply take a little longer.

faqs.q: Are my files uploaded?
faqs.a: No. Every PDF is converted locally in your browser and never sent to a server, which keeps even confidential documents private.

faqs.q: How do I download everything?
faqs.a: Once files finish converting, click "Download all as ZIP" to get a single archive containing one .md file per PDF.

faqs.q: Can I preview individual files?
faqs.a: Yes. Select any file in the queue to see its Markdown in preview or source view, and copy or download it on its own.

features.title: Privacy first
features.body: The best converter keeps files on your device. Ours runs 100% in the browser — no uploads, no accounts, no tracking.

features.title: Real structure
features.body: Look beyond raw text: good tools preserve headings, lists and true Markdown tables. Ours keeps your document's structure intact.

features.title: Handles hard cases
features.body: Scanned PDFs, embedded images and dozens of files at once — a great tool covers them with OCR, image extraction and batch mode.

faqs.q: What is the best PDF to Markdown converter?
faqs.a: The best converter is the one that keeps your document structure, respects your privacy, and handles your file types. A browser-based tool that needs no upload or install — with tables, images, OCR and batch support — covers most needs for free.

faqs.q: What is the best free PDF to Markdown converter?
faqs.a: This tool is free with no limits and runs entirely in your browser. Unlike many free online converters, it never uploads your files, and it supports tables, images, scanned PDFs and batch conversion.

faqs.q: Are online PDF to Markdown converters safe?
faqs.a: It depends on whether they upload your file. Many do, which is risky for confidential documents. This converter processes everything locally in your browser, so your files never leave your device.

faqs.q: Do I need to install software?
faqs.a: No. Command-line tools like Pandoc are powerful but require installation and setup. This converter works instantly in any modern browser, on any device.

features.title: Keeps your images
features.body: Photos, charts and diagrams are pulled out of the PDF and inlined in the Markdown, positioned where they appear.

features.title: Self-contained .md
features.body: Images are embedded as base64 data URLs, so the Markdown file works anywhere with no separate image folder.

features.title: 100% private
features.body: Parsing and image extraction run locally via WebAssembly and canvas. Your PDF never leaves your device.

faqs.q: How are images kept during conversion?
faqs.a: The converter detects where each image is drawn on the page, crops it from a high-resolution render, and inlines it in the Markdown at the matching position.

faqs.q: Are the images uploaded anywhere?
faqs.a: No. Everything happens in your browser — the PDF and its images are processed locally and never sent to a server.

faqs.q: What format are the extracted images?
faqs.a: Images are embedded as PNG data URLs directly in the Markdown, so a single .md file contains both text and pictures.

faqs.q: Will very small icons be included?
faqs.a: Tiny graphics like bullets and hairline rules are skipped, so you get the meaningful figures and charts rather than UI clutter.

MdToPdf.sampleContent:
# Project Brief

Welcome to **Markdown to PDF**. Edit on the left — the preview on the right updates instantly.

## Features

- Clean, readable typography
- Tables, lists and code blocks
- Runs 100% in your browser

## Example table

| Feature | Supported |
| --- | --- |
| Headings | Yes |
| Tables | Yes |
| Code | Yes |

## Code

```js
console.log("hello, pdf");
```

> Tip: click "Download PDF" and choose "Save as PDF" to export this document.

features.title: Live preview
features.body: See exactly how your PDF will look as you type. Headings, tables, lists and code render instantly.

features.title: 100% private
features.body: Conversion runs entirely in your browser. Your Markdown never leaves your device.

features.title: Clean typography
features.body: Professional spacing, readable fonts and styled tables and code blocks — ready to share or print.

faqs.q: How do I convert Markdown to PDF?
faqs.a: Write or paste your Markdown on the left, check the live preview on the right, then click Download PDF and choose "Save as PDF" in the print dialog.

faqs.q: Is it free and private?
faqs.a: Yes. The tool is completely free and runs entirely in your browser — your content is never uploaded to any server.

faqs.q: Does it support tables and code blocks?
faqs.a: Yes. GitHub-flavored Markdown is supported, including tables, task lists, fenced code blocks, bold text and links.

faqs.q: Can I choose the page size?
faqs.a: Yes. Switch between A4 and Letter before exporting your PDF.

Ocr.metaTitle: Scanned PDF to Markdown — Free OCR Converter
Ocr.metaDescription: Turn scanned or image-only PDFs into Markdown with built-in OCR. Recognizes text in 100+ languages, runs in your browser, and your file is never uploaded.

Ocr.modelNote: First run downloads a language model (~2–15MB). After that it works offline — your file is processed locally.
Ocr.errorTooLarge: File exceeds 50MB. Larger files are not supported yet.
Ocr.errorNoPages: Could not read any pages from this PDF.
Ocr.errorFailed: OCR failed. Please try another file.
Ocr.howToName: How to convert a scanned PDF to Markdown
howToSteps: Choose the document language, then drop your scanned or image-only PDF.
Watch each page get rendered and recognized in your browser, side by side.
Copy the Markdown or download the .md file once OCR finishes.

features.title: Built-in OCR
features.body: Reads text from scanned and image-only PDFs that normal converters can't, powered by Tesseract in your browser.

features.title: 100+ languages
features.body: Recognize English, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Spanish, French, German, Russian and many more.

features.title: Private by design
features.body: OCR runs locally via WebAssembly. Only the language model is fetched — your document never leaves your device.

faqs.q: What is a scanned PDF?
faqs.a: A scanned or image-only PDF has no selectable text layer — it's essentially pictures of pages. Regular converters return nothing, so OCR is needed to read the text.

faqs.q: Is the OCR free and private?
faqs.a: Yes. Recognition runs entirely in your browser using WebAssembly. Only the language model is downloaded from a CDN; your PDF is never uploaded.

faqs.q: Which languages are supported?
faqs.a: Pick from English, Simplified and Traditional Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Spanish, French, German, Russian and more before you start.

faqs.q: Why is OCR slower than a normal conversion?
faqs.a: OCR analyzes each page image pixel by pixel to recognize characters, which is heavier than reading an existing text layer. Larger documents take longer.

Footer.tagline: PDF to Markdown · all conversions run locally in your browser, your files never leave your device.

general.resultPreview:
# Getting Started with RAG

Retrieval augmented generation combines a language model with an external knowledge base.

## Why Markdown

Markdown is compact and structured, which makes it the preferred input format for large language models.

## Next Steps

Convert your source documents to Markdown, chunk them, and store the embeddings in a vector database.

obsidian.howToName: How to import a PDF into Obsidian as Markdown
steps: Upload your research notes PDF, or click "Try sample PDF" to load our demo notes file.
Download the .md file — it includes YAML frontmatter (title, date, tags) ready for your vault.
Move the file into any folder in your Obsidian vault. It appears instantly as a structured note.

python.resultPreview:
# Research Summary

## Overview

This document was converted from PDF to Markdown for use in Python scripts and notebooks.

## Key points

- Structured headings map cleanly to section parsers
- Tables export as GFM for pandas or tabulate
- Local conversion keeps sensitive PDFs off shared servers

Guide.metaTitle: How to Convert PDF to Markdown — Free Step-by-Step Guide
Guide.metaDescription: Learn how to convert PDF to Markdown in 3 steps. Free browser-based converter — no upload, no account. Works with text-based PDFs for LLM, Obsidian and docs.
keywords: how to convert pdf to markdown
pdf to markdown step by step
Guide.h1: How to Convert PDF to Markdown
Guide.subtitle: Turn any text-based PDF into clean Markdown in your browser — free, private, and ready in seconds. Use the converter below and follow the steps.

features.title: 1. Upload your PDF
features.body: Drag and drop a text-based PDF onto the converter above, or click to browse. Files up to 50 MB are supported. Try the sample PDF if you want to see the output first.

features.title: 2. Review the Markdown
features.body: Switch between Preview and Source view to check headings, lists, and tables. Edit is not built in yet — copy the output into your editor if you need to tweak it.

features.title: 3. Copy or download
features.body: Click Copy Markdown to paste into ChatGPT, Obsidian, or a code editor. Or download the .md file and move it into your vault, repo, or RAG pipeline.

faqs.q: What type of PDF works best?
faqs.a: Text-based PDFs — reports, papers, ebooks exported from Word or Google Docs — convert most reliably. Scanned or image-only PDFs need OCR, which is on our roadmap.

faqs.q: Is conversion really local?
faqs.a: Yes. Parsing and Markdown generation run entirely in your browser via WebAssembly. Your PDF is never uploaded to our servers.

faqs.q: Why use Markdown instead of copying text from the PDF?
faqs.a: Copy-paste often breaks table columns, loses heading hierarchy, and includes page headers. Structured Markdown preserves document layout with fewer tokens — ideal for LLMs and note apps.

faqs.q: Can I convert multiple PDFs at once?
faqs.a: Not yet. Batch conversion is planned for a future release. For now, convert files one at a time — each run is free and unlimited.

faqs.q: How do I import the result into Obsidian?
faqs.a: Click “Download .md” and move the file into any folder inside your Obsidian vault. It appears instantly as a new note.

faqs.q: Will tables and lists survive the import?
faqs.a: Yes. Tables are converted to GitHub-flavored Markdown tables and nested bullet lists keep their indentation.

features.title: Token-efficient
features.body: Markdown is far more compact than raw PDF text dumps, so you fit more context into the model’s window.

features.title: Structure the model understands
features.body: Clean headings and lists give the model document hierarchy, which improves grounding and reduces hallucination.

features.body: Predictable Markdown is easy to chunk and embed into a vector database for retrieval augmented generation.

faqs.q: Are my files uploaded to a server?
faqs.a: No. Conversion runs entirely in your browser using WebAssembly. Your PDF never leaves your device, which makes it safe for contracts, research and other private documents.

faqs.q: Why is Markdown better than raw PDF text for LLMs?
faqs.a: Markdown encodes structure (headings, lists, tables) with very few extra tokens, which helps the model interpret the document and keeps prompts small.

faqs.q: Can I use the output in a RAG pipeline?
faqs.a: Yes. The clean Markdown is straightforward to split into chunks and embed for retrieval, with headings acting as natural chunk boundaries.

features.title: Automatic column detection
features.body: The engine aligns cells by their horizontal position, so multi-column tables come out correctly structured.

features.title: GFM tables
features.body: Output uses standard GitHub-flavored Markdown tables that render in GitHub, Obsidian, Notion and most editors.

faqs.q: Are my files uploaded to a server?

faqs.q: What table layouts are supported?
faqs.a: Text-based tables with clear column spacing convert best. Merged cells are flattened because Markdown tables don’t support cell spanning.

faqs.q: Can I copy just the table?

Python.metaTitle: PDF to Markdown Python — Browser Tool & Script Tips
Python.metaDescription: Convert PDF to Markdown without writing Python first — free local browser tool. Plus tips for pymupdf, pdfplumber and our upcoming API for automation.
Python.subtitle: Need Markdown from a PDF for a notebook, ETL job or prototype? Convert locally in the browser, then copy the string into your Python workflow — no environment setup.

features.title: Zero-setup conversion
features.body: Skip installing pymupdf, pdfplumber or Java dependencies for one-off files. Upload, copy the Markdown string, and paste into your script.

features.title: Structured for parsing
features.body: Predictable heading levels and GFM tables make it easy to split sections with regex or feed into LangChain, LlamaIndex and other Python RAG stacks.

features.title: API coming soon
features.body: Batch conversion and a REST API for programmatic access are on the roadmap. Contact us if you want early access for production pipelines.

faqs.q: Is there a Python library or pip package?
faqs.a: This site is a browser-based converter, not a pip module. For quick one-file jobs, use the tool above. For fully programmatic pipelines, common libraries include PyMuPDF, pdfplumber and marker — or wait for our upcoming API.

faqs.q: Can I use the output in Jupyter or pandas?
faqs.a: Yes. Copy the Markdown into a notebook cell for documentation, or parse GFM tables into DataFrames with pandas.read_table on the table blocks.

faqs.q: Does this replace a Python script in production?
faqs.a: For ad-hoc and prototype work, the browser tool is faster to start. For automated batch jobs in CI or cron, a script or API is better — our API is planned for that use case.

PrivacyPolicy.metaDescription: Learn how PDF to Markdown handles your data. PDF conversion runs locally in your browser; we use Google Analytics and Microsoft Clarity for site usage insights.
PrivacyPolicy.intro: PDF to Markdown (pdftomarkdown.run) is a free browser-based tool that converts PDF files to Markdown. This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and the choices available to you.

sections.title: 1. PDF conversion — your files stay on your device
paragraphs: When you convert a PDF, all processing happens entirely in your web browser using WebAssembly. Your PDF files are never uploaded to our servers or any third-party service for conversion.
We do not store, access, or retain the content of your PDF files or the Markdown output you generate. Once you close the browser tab, that data is gone from our perspective.

sections.title: 2. Information we collect automatically
paragraphs: When you visit our website, we and our analytics partners may automatically collect certain technical and usage information, such as:
bullets: Pages visited, links clicked, and time spent on the site
Browser type, operating system, device type, and screen resolution
Approximate geographic location (country/region level, derived from IP address)
Referring website or search query
Session interactions such as scrolls, clicks, and mouse movements (via Microsoft Clarity)

sections.title: 3. Analytics services we use
paragraphs: We use the following third-party analytics tools to understand how visitors use our site and to improve the product. These services may set cookies or similar technologies in your browser.
bullets: Google Analytics (GA4) — provided by Google LLC. Google Analytics collects data about how you interact with our website, including pages viewed and events triggered. Data is processed according to Google's privacy policy. You can learn more at https://policies.google.com/privacy and opt out via the Google Analytics Opt-out Browser Add-on at https://tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout.

Microsoft Clarity — provided by Microsoft Corporation. Clarity uses cookies and similar technologies to record session replays, heatmaps, and interaction data to help us understand user experience and fix usability issues. Data is processed according to Microsoft's privacy policy. You can learn more at https://privacy.microsoft.com/en-us/privacystatement.

sections.title: 4. Cookies and similar technologies
paragraphs: Our analytics providers use cookies, local storage, and similar tracking technologies to collect usage data described above. You can control cookies through your browser settings — most browsers allow you to block or delete cookies.
Disabling cookies may not affect the core PDF conversion functionality, which runs entirely in your browser without requiring cookies.

sections.title: 5. How we use collected information
paragraphs: We use the automatically collected information to:
bullets: Monitor site performance and fix errors
Understand which features and pages are most useful
Improve the user experience and prioritize product development
Detect and prevent abuse or fraudulent activity

sections.title: 6. Data sharing
paragraphs: We do not sell your personal information. Usage data collected by Google Analytics and Microsoft Clarity is shared with those respective providers as described in their privacy policies.
We may also disclose information if required by law, court order, or to protect the rights, property, or safety of our users or the public.

sections.title: 7. Data retention
paragraphs: Analytics data is retained according to the retention policies of Google Analytics and Microsoft Clarity. PDF files and conversion output are never stored on our servers.

sections.title: 8. Your rights and choices
paragraphs: Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, correct, or delete personal data held about you. You can also:
bullets: Opt out of Google Analytics using the browser add-on linked above
Block or delete cookies via your browser settings
Use a privacy-focused browser or extensions to limit tracking

sections.title: 9. Children's privacy
paragraphs: Our service is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children.

sections.title: 10. Changes to this policy
paragraphs: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we do, we will revise the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. Continued use of the site after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

sections.title: 11. Contact
paragraphs: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at privacy@pdftomarkdown.run.

About.metaTitle: About PDF to Markdown — Free Local Converter
About.metaDescription: Learn about PDF to Markdown (pdftomarkdown.run): a free, browser-based PDF to Markdown tool built for privacy, LLM workflows, and knowledge management.
About.intro: PDF to Markdown (pdftomarkdown.run) is a free online tool that converts PDF files to clean Markdown entirely in your browser. No uploads, no account required.

sections.title: What we do
paragraphs: We help developers, researchers, and knowledge workers turn PDF documents into structured Markdown ready for LLMs, RAG pipelines, Obsidian, Notion, and other tools.
Our converter preserves headings, lists, nested bullets, and tables while stripping page numbers and repeated headers so the output is clean and token-efficient.

sections.title: Why we built it
paragraphs: Most online PDF converters require uploading files to a server, which is a dealbreaker for contracts, internal reports, and research notes.

Existing tools often produce messy Markdown that breaks tables and wastes tokens when fed to language models. We built PDF to Markdown to solve both problems: keep files on your device and produce output designed for modern AI and note-taking workflows.

sections.title: How it works
paragraphs: When you drop a PDF, parsing and conversion run locally in your browser using WebAssembly. Your file is never transmitted to our servers.
We use Google Analytics and Microsoft Clarity only to understand general site usage, not to access your documents.
bullets: 100% local PDF processing in the browser
No sign-up, no watermark, no daily limit for single files
Works offline once the page is loaded

sections.title: What's next
paragraphs: We are actively improving conversion quality and adding capabilities based on user feedback.
bullets: OCR support for scanned and image-only PDFs
Batch conversion for multiple files
Higher-fidelity formula and table handling
A developer API for automation and RAG pipelines

sections.title: Get in touch
paragraphs: Questions, feedback, or partnership ideas? Head to our contact page — we read every message.

Contact.metaTitle: Contact PDF to Markdown — Get in Touch
Contact.metaDescription: Contact the PDF to Markdown team for feedback, bug reports, privacy questions, or partnership inquiries. We typically respond within 2–3 business days.
Contact.intro: We would love to hear from you. Whether you have product feedback, found a conversion issue, or want to explore a partnership, send us an email and we will get back to you.
Contact.responseTime: We typically respond within 2–3 business days.

sections.title: What we can help with
paragraphs: PDF to Markdown is a small, focused tool. Here are the most common reasons people reach out:
bullets: Bug reports or unexpected conversion results
Feature requests (OCR, batch conversion, API access)
Feedback on output quality for specific PDF types
Partnership, integration, or press inquiries
Privacy and data-handling questions

sections.title: Before you write
paragraphs: Your PDF files are processed locally in your browser — we cannot see or recover them. If you are reporting a conversion issue, please describe the problem and, if possible, note the PDF type (scanned, multi-column, heavy tables, etc.). Do not attach sensitive documents unless you are comfortable sharing them.
For privacy-specific requests (data access, deletion, or cookie questions), mention "Privacy request" in the subject line so we can prioritize your message.

sections.title: Privacy & data requests
paragraphs: PDF conversion does not upload your files to our servers. Analytics data is described in our Privacy Policy. For any privacy-related question, use the same email address above.
